title: Azedarach excelsa
taxonomy: Azedarach excelsa is a species of tree in the family Meliaceae, commonly known as the ironwood tree. It is native to Australia and is also found in other parts of the world. The tree is a member of the genus Azedarach, which consists of several species of flowering plants. Azedarach excelsa is often classified as a deciduous tree, meaning it sheds its leaves seasonally. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted as a member of the Meliaceae family.
uses: Azedarach excelsa, a plant native to Australia and Asia, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant's leaves, bark, and seeds have been used to treat various health issues. In some traditional medicine, Azedarach excelsa is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reducing fever. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been studied for their potential to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. More research is needed to fully understand the extent of Azedarach excelsa's medicinal uses.
